meta.title: הלב שלי
meta.author: אייל גולן
meta.note: Positions verified via getBoundingClientRect() pixel measurement against the live Tab4U page. Same title as the Ishay Ribo song, but a different song/artist — added specifically to catch the DOM-order reversal regression (first chord in source = last word, not first). Every position below (including middle chords of 3-chord lines) is an exact word match to the live page: the unified proportional formula in tab4uTokensToLyricPositions scales each chord's distance-from-right by the lyric's own width (lyricFieldWidth), which lands middle chords on the correct word (Db on ולא / פינה).

lines.lyricsContains: מביט בך מאושר ולא אומר דבר
ולא יכול כי זה חזק ממני
lines.note: Dense cluster: the source chord row packs Db/B/Bb into columns 1-8 (over the phrase's last word ממני@19) with a lone B far right (over ولا@0). The unified formula lands all three on ~19; parseTextToStructuredSong's de-collision then spreads them to 19/22/23. Exact per-chord pixel targets are inherently ambiguous here, so this line asserts the rendered (de-collided) result, not a pure Tab4U position.
